channel.title: Jenkov.com - News
channel.description: News from Jenkov.com about new tech tutorials, videos, products etc.

item.title: Scoped access in Java
item.description: I have published a tutorial about scoped access in Java. There is a video version of the tutorial too - linked from the textual version of the tutorial.

item.title: Method Chaining in Java
item.description: I have published a tutorial about method chaining in Java. There is a video version of the tutorial too - linked from the textual version of the tutorial.

item.title: Java ConcurrentMap tutorial - updated
item.description: I have udpated my Java ConcurrentMap tutorial - and added a video version of the tutorial.

item.title: What is new in Java 23
item.description: I have updated my Java tutorial introduction to list what is new in Java 23. The list of new features now lists all the major features in each release, back to Java 7

item.title: Help Your Co-developers Grow - By Meeting Them Where They Are
item.description: I have published a short video about helping your co-developers grow - by meeting them where they are and helping them grow from there. This advice may sound benign, but I have seen how well it works when done well, and I have also seen how poorly it works when developers ignore this advice, and scorn their co-developers for not having the "sufficient" knowledge level.

item.title: Java HttpClient - Updated
item.description: I have updated my Java HttpClient tutorial to also show how to send an HTTP POST request to a web server.

item.title: P2P Networks Book Update
item.description: I have updated my book about P2P Networks with a short chapter about the Kademlia P2P topology, and a few more details about Chord. More to come in the future.

item.title: Compositional Software Design
item.description: I have finally "figured out" the core principles of what I call Compositional Software Design. I have published a tutorial explaining these core principles.There is still more to say about Compositional Software Design than what is currently in this tutorial - but there is enough to understand the central ideas. More will be added in the future, but will probably mostly be more nuances and details around the core principles.

item.title: Daemon Threads in java
item.description: I have just updated my tutorial about creating threads in Java, so it now also explains how to create Daemon threads.

item.title: Designing for Scalability vs. Performance
item.description: I have made a short video about the difference between designing for scalability or performance. In other words, should you solve all capacity problems by throwing hardware at the problem? Or should you try to utilize the hardware you already have, better?

item.title: HTML5 Canvas : Images - Fixed
item.description: I have fixed my HTML5 Canvas : Images tutorial - so the examples are actually drawn.

item.title: Java Generics - Updated
item.description: I have updated my Java Generics introduction just now. Java Generics enables you to type safely create classes that can work on multiple different types.

item.title: HTML5 Canvas - Rectangles - Updated
item.description: I have just fixed a small bug in the code examples in my HTML5 Canvas Rectangles tutorial - about how to draw rectangles on an HTML5 Canvas.

item.title: Python Classes
item.description: I have just published a short tutorial on Python classes.

item.title: Java Generics Tutorial Video
item.description: I have just published a video about Java Generics

item.title: Java HttpClient
item.description: The Java HttpClient was added in Java 11 and provides an HTTP 1.1 and 2.0 client built into the Java core libraries. In this Java HttpClient tutorial I explain the basics of how to use the HttpClient.

item.title: Java 21 and 22 Features
item.description: I have updated my Java introduction to list the major new features of Java 21 and 22. The page now lists all major features all the way back to Java 7.

item.title: Java Generic Methods - Updated
item.description: I have clarified the explanations in my Java Generic Methods tutorial - so it is easier to understand what you can do.

item.title: Search Function on Jenkov.com
item.description: I have added a search function to jenkov.com - after many requests to do so. It works well enough for you to be able to find most major topics on jenkov.com - and I keep improving it so you can find more and more. Try it out!

item.title: P2P Networks Book - Available
item.description: My book about P2P Networks is in progress, and is available on LeanPub. It has actually been available a while now - I just forgot to announce it. Payment is optional.

item.title: P2P Networks Introduction - Updated
item.description: I have updated my P2P networks introduction to give a better overview of the core concepts and ideas.

item.title: Scalability vs. Performance?
item.description: I have updated my Performance Optimization Principles tutorial to include a section discussing the difference between designing for scalability and performance - and which of the two approaches I prefer.

item.title: Java Ternary Operator Tutorial - updated
item.description: The Java ternary operator lets you express conditional value assignment more concisely than by using if-statements. I have updated my Java ternary operator tutorial to also show how to use the ternary operator as a type check.

item.title: Python while Loop Tutorial
item.description: I have published a Python while loop tutorial. While loops enable you to repeat a block of code as long as a certain condition is true.

item.title: Java ForkJoinPool Tutorial Updated
item.description: I have updated my Java ForkJoinPool tutorial with simpler code examples, and a link to the Java ForkJoinPool video tutorial.

item.title: Java ForkJoinPool Video
item.description: I've published a video version of my Java ForkJoinPool tutorial. The Java ForkJoinPool makes it easier to break down big tasks into smaller tasks which can be executed in parallel.

item.title: Python for Loop
item.description: I've published a short tutorial on the Python for loop which is used to iterate sequences of elements such as lists, sets, tuples, dictionaries, ranges etc.

item.title: Python if
item.description: I've published a short tutorial on the Python if instruction which is used to execute one or more instructions if a certain condition is met.

item.title: Python Data Types
item.description: I've published a short tutorial on the core Python data types such as boolean, numeric, string, list, tuple, dictionary etc.

item.title: Python Variables
item.description: I've published a short tutorial on the Python variables - about how to declare, read and set their values.

item.title: P2P Networks Introduction - Video
item.description: I have published a P2P Networks Introduction video - covering some of the things that have happened since my first video about P2P networks 12 years ago. This includes different P2P topologies among other things - including my own Polymorph Polyring P2P topology.

item.title: Polymorph Data Language
item.description: Polymorph Data Language is my own alternative to JSON, YAML, XML, CSV etc. It has most of their features + several new features combined into a single data language - having all the features I have alwways wanted a data language to have.

item.title: Java Concurrency Introduction - Updated
item.description: Java gets new concurrency and multithreading constructs added regularly. Latest additions were Java Virtual Threads and Structured Concurrency. I will keep updating this tutorial accordingly.

item.title: Jackson ObjectMapper - Updated
item.description: Smaller updates to my tutorial about the Jackson ObjectMapper - which can parse JSON into Java objects and vice versa.

item.title: Java Reflection - Finding Getters and Setters of a Class at Runtime
item.description: It is possible to detect getters and setters of a Java class at runtime via Java Reflection. There are no direct methods for finding getters and setters built-in to the Java Reflection API, but you can still use it to detect getters and setters. I have updated this tutorial to be a bit more clear about how you do that!

item.title: Single Process Architecture
item.description: I have updated my tutorial explaining single process architecture a bit - to be more clear about whether network communication makes an app not have a single process architecture or not.

item.title: Conscious Software Design - Video
item.description: I have made a video explanation of my software design philsophy Conscious Software Design. In this video I go into more detail about what exactly this philosophy is all about. Hint: It's more about how to think about software design than about concrete techniques.

item.title: Java Microbenchmark Harness - Tutorial
item.description: If you are doing performance optimization in Java, sometimes you will need to write some "microbenchmarks" that measure how fast a given implementation runs. Doing that so that the benchmarks actually measures the implementation fairly - is not easy! The Java Microbenchmark Harness can help you out here! This Java Microbenchmark Harness tutorial explains how to use it, and how to write micro benchmarks.

item.title: Conscious Software Design - Consciousness Levels
item.description: I have updated my tutorial about conscious software design to include a list of 4 different conscioussness levels I believe developers and architects tend to operate on: Oblivious, unconscious, subconscious and conscious.

item.title: Conscious Software Design
item.description: Here are what I currently believe to be the core principles of what I call "Conscious Software Design". More details will be added over time, but I believe these three principles to be what is most important in this philosophy.

item.title: The SOLID Principles
item.description: Here are my notes about the SOLID design principles so far. I will probably be adding some comments to them over time, as I learn more about software design - but here is the first version.

item.title: Structural Software Design
item.description: I have just published a short tutorial where I lay out the basics of structural software design. There is a video version of the tutorial too - linked from inside the tutorial.

item.title: What is new in Java 20?
item.description: I have updated my Java introduction to list the new features in Java 20. This Java introduction now lists all the big new features in Java, all the way back to Java 7. If you are not sure if you missed a feature, you can check this list.

item.title: Software Design Goals
item.description: New article about software design goals - the commonly occurring reasons behind why we make the design decisions we do. There is both a textual and a video version of the article. There is a link to the video from within the article.

item.title: Software Design Introduction
item.description: I have finished my software design introduction - laying out what I believe to be the fundamental concepts of software design. More tutorials with more details will come in the future. There is a textual and video version.

item.title: Java volatile Tutorial - Corrected
item.description: I have corrected my Java volatile tutorial - so that it no longer talks about the "CPU cache" - but "CPU registers" instead. Both text and diagrams have been fixed ?

item.title: Undertow Java Web Server Introduction
item.description: I have published a short introduction to the Undertow Java web server. Undertow is the web server used internally in JBoss Wildfly. Undertow is a minmalistic web server which can be embedded into your own Java applications. You can package your project as a single fat jar, from as little as 5-6 MB . Undertow has great performance, offers both a non-blocking (single-threaded) and a blocking IO model, and is quite easy to use. Check it out! ?

item.title: The Compound Performance Penalty Problem - Video Edition
item.description: The Compound Performance Penalty Problem - about how using more and more third party software in your apps will often come with a performance penalty. With many third party libs - that penalty compounds.

item.title: ExecutorService Using Java Virtual Threads Internally - Video Edition
item.description: I have created a video showing how to create a Java ExecutorService that uses virtual threads internally.

item.title: ExecutorService Using Java Virtual Threads Internally
item.description: I have updated my Java ExecutorService tutorial to show how to create an ExecutorService that uses virtual threads internally.

item.title: Java Virtual Threads - Video Edition
item.description: I have published a video version of my Java Virtual Threads tutorial.

item.title: Java Virtual Threads
item.description: I have published my first notes on Java virtual threads. Enough to get you started playing with them. More will come later!

item.title: Programming Paradigms
item.description: I have collected a list of the most common programming paradigms I have encountered in my 20+ years of professional software development. This list contains paradigms such as structured programming, functional programming, object oriented programming, data oriented programming etc.

item.title: How to Choose What Programming Languages to Learn?
item.description: With all the programming languages available out there today, it can be hard to choose which programming languages to learn! Therefore I have published my own criteria for choosing what programming languages I choose to learn myself.

item.title: New Features in Java 19
item.description: I have just updated my Java introduction to list the new features in Java 19. The Java introduction now lists almost all the new features added in each new Java version, going back to Java 7. Do you know about them all?

item.title: Java Thread Signaling - Updated With Video
item.description: I have updated my Java Thread signaling tutorial, among other things with a video version of the tutorial.

item.title: Evolutionary Software Development
item.description: Evolutionary software development is the term I am using for the development process I see many startups and also larger corporations actually use, even though they call it Scrum or Kanban on the surface. This article explains the three core principles of evolutionary software development.

item.title: UFT-8 Tutorial Updated
item.description: I have updated my UTF-8 tutorial to also show how to read and write UTF-8 in Java.

item.title: Unicode Tutorial Cleaned Up
item.description: I have cleaned up my Unicode tutorial so it is now easier to get an overview of Unicode.

item.title: AWS CloudFormation Parameters
item.description: AWS CloudFormation templates can have parameters which can be filled in at the time the CloudFormation template is deployed, making it possible to use the same AWS CloudFormation template to create different (but similar) stacks.

item.title: AWS CloudFormation Introduction
item.description: I have started writing about AWS CloudFormation which is one of the AWS IaC (Infrastructure as Code) tools made available for free by AWS.

item.title: Java Generics - Updated
item.description: I have started updating my Java Generics tutorial

item.title: AWS Core Technologies
item.description: With hundreds of AWS services and technologies available, it can be hard to figure out which technologies to learn first, and which can wait. Therefore I have published a list of what I consider core AWS technologies which I believe it is good to know early in your AWS journey. These technologies are often used, so learning them will be beneficial.

item.title: AWS Introduction
item.description: If you are just beginning your AWS journey - figuring out which AWS services to learn first, and how to interact best with AWS can be a daunting task. Therefore I have published a short introduction to AWS which lists the bare essentials to guide you on this AWS journey.

item.title: Core Software Performance Optimization Principles
item.description: This tutorial lists the core 9 + 1 software performance optimization principles I use when working with software performance optimization.

item.title: Developer Learning Strategies
item.description: As developer we have to learn a lot continuously. Learning can sometimes be frustrating. I have published an article explaining my developer learning strategies - for dealing with the times when learning is hard.

item.title: P2P Network Introduction Updated
item.description: I have updated my P2P network introduction with information about structured and unstructured P2P network topologies (such as Gnutella).

item.title: HTML5 Tutorial - Introduction Fixed
item.description: I have fixed a small error in the introduction to the HTML5 Canvas - the powerful HTML5 element that enables you to draw free form graphics directly in the browser.

item.title: Essential Dev Skills
item.description: I have made a list of skills / topics I believe most developers will benefit from having / knowing about to some extent at least.

item.title: What is new in Java 18?
item.description: I have updated the Java version / feature list to list what is new in Java 18.

item.title: What is new in Java 17?
item.description: I have updated the Java version / feature list to list what is new in Java 17.

item.title: What is new in Java 16?
item.description: I have updated the Java version / feature list to list what is new in Java 16.

item.title: SVG Tutorials Moved
item.description: I have moved my SVG tutorials from tutorials.jenkov.com to jenkov.com

item.title: CSS Tutorials Moved
item.description: I have moved my CSS tutorials from tutorials.jenkov.com to jenkov.com

item.title: Maven Tutorials Moved
item.description: I have moved my Maven tutorials from tutorials.jenkov.com to jenkov.com

item.title: Peer-to-peer Tutorials Moved
item.description: I have moved my Peer-to-Peer (P2P) tutorials from tutorials.jenkov.com to jenkov.com

item.title: GraalVM JavaScript Interpreter
item.description: GraalVM comes with a JavaScript interpreter which you can use to run JavaScript both from the command line and from inside your Java code. GraalVM also has Node.js + NPM compatible commands you can install - if you want or need that. In this tutorial I explain how to use the GraalVM JavaScript interpreter, and how to install the Node.JS + NPM commands.

item.title: Polymorph Introduction
item.description: Polymorph is my own project within the smart, decentralized media space. The project is now concrete enough that I was able to write an introduction to what Polymorph is, and explain the bigger picture of what it will do and how it will work.

item.title: Decentralized Systems - Updated
item.description: I have updated the tutorial on decentralized systems to also list some of the most common benefits and challenges of decentralized systems.

item.title: Decentralized Systems
item.description: I have published a short introduction to decentralized systems such as decentralized server systems like DHT (Distributed Hashtables), P2P networks, Blockchain systems etc.

item.title: GraalVM Python Interpreter
item.description: GraalVM has a Python interpreter you can install which enables you to run Python programs on GraalVM or execute Python from within your Java code. This short tutorial explains how to install the GraalVM Python interpreter - and how to run a Python program from the command line.

item.title: GraalVM Polyglot API
item.description: I have added 2 small examples of how to execute JavaScript from inside a Java app via the GraalVM Polyglot API.

item.title: GraalVM Tutorial
item.description: I have started writing a tutorial about GraalVM - the VM that can run Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, LLVM and Web Assembly in the same application.

item.title: Blockchain Projects and Resources
item.description: I have started making a list of the most interesting and popular blockchain projects out there, to help beginners get started on their crypto journeys.

item.title: Blockchain Introduction
item.description: Blockchain technology is seeing an increase in use cases it can address, in addition to just crypto currency. Therefore I have started studying blockchain technology, and also started writing about it. So far I have published a short introduction to blockchain. More will follow in the future.

item.title: Thread Congestion
item.description: If multiple threads try to access the same, blocking data structure at the same time, often, this can lead to thread congestion, which may lead to performance degradation.

item.title: Single-threaded and Same-threaded Designs
item.description: Single-threaded and same-threaded designs can be very powerful if you understand how to design them properly. I have updated this tutorial with a video edition.

item.title: Polymorph P2P Network Algorithm
item.description: The Polymorph P2P network algorithm is based on my own, new P2P polyring topology designed especially with media routing in mind. I believe it is better suited at media routing than Chord, Kademlia, Pastry and Taspestry.

item.title: Chord P2P Network Algorithm
item.description: The Chord P2P network algorithm was one of the early P2P algorithms to appear. This Chord tutorial explains the core of the Chord P2P algorithm.

item.title: Java Annotations
item.description: I have updated my Java Annotations tutorial to also explain the built-in annotation @Contended.

item.title: False Sharing in Java
item.description: False sharing is a situation that can cause performance degradations when two or more threads write to different variables located within the same CPU cache line.

item.title: CPU Cache Coherence in Java Concurrency
item.description: In this short tutorial I correct my statements about how Java synchronized and volatile variables effect how thread visible variables are flushed to main RAM.

item.title: Compare and Swap in Java
item.description: Compare and swap functionality is an alternative to synchronized blocks in Java when designing high throughput, non-blocking concurrent algorithms in Java.

item.title: Peer Findability and Message Routability
item.description: The peer findability and message routability challenges of P2P networks are typically addressed in similar ways. This tutorial covers common solutions to peer findability and message routability in peer-to-peer networks.

item.title: Peer Addressability
item.description: Peers in a peer-to-peer network need to be able to address each other uniquely. That it was peer addressability refers to. This tutorial explains how common P2P algorithms approaches peer addressability.

item.title: Peer Connectivity
item.description: Peer connectivity tutorial out! Explains a common solution to overcoming peers behind NAT and firewall - so all peers in a P2P network can communicate with each other.

item.title: Peer-to-peer Networks - Update Started
item.description: A lot has happened in the world of Peer-to-Peer (P2P) networks since I wrote the first version of this tutorial - so I have started updating it. First step was updating the introduction.

item.title: Single-threaded Concurrency
item.description: Single-threaded concurrency designs are an alternative to multithreaded concurrency designs which achieves many of the same benefits (task switching) without many of the multithreading problems (thread update visibility, race conditions etc.etc.).

item.title: Java BlockingQueue
item.description: The Java BlockingQueue interface represents a queue which can block threads from inserting into a full queue, or block threads from dequeuing from an empty queue.

item.title: Deadlock Prevention in Java
item.description: In this tutorial I explain 3 common methods for preventing deadlock occurring. I have also made a video version of this tutorial.

item.title: Deadlock in Java
item.description: Deadlock is when two or more threads end up blocked indefinitely waiting for each other to release resources they need to lock to do their job. I have updated the Deadlock tutorial.

item.title: Java ExecutorService
item.description: The Java ExecutorService is a thread pool built into Java. I have update the Java ExecutorService tutorial, including added a new video version of this tutorial.

item.title: JavaFX TableView - Editable Cells
item.description: I have updated my JavaFX TableView tutorial to explain how to make table cells editable.

item.title: Java Lock
item.description: The Java Lock interface represents an lock which can make sure only one thread can lock it at a time - similarly to how a Java synchronized blocks works - only more flexible.

item.title: Thread Pools in Java
item.description: Thread pools are an alternative to creative one thread per task to execute. In this thread pool tutorial I explain how thread pools work, as well as how to implement your own in Java. I have also added a video version of this tutorial.

item.title: JavaFX Transformations
item.description: You can apply transformations such as rotation, translation and scaling to all JavaFX controls. This JavaFX transformations tutorial explains how JavaFX transformations work.

item.title: JavaFX Button
item.description: The JavaFX Button class provides a button that users of your JavaFX app can click on. I have updated my JavaFX Button tutorial to explain how to use a Button from within JavaFX FXML.

item.title: Concurrency vs Parallelism
item.description: Concurrency and parallelism are two similar, yet different concepts. I have rewritten this tutorial to better explain the difference between concurrency and parallelism - plus I have added a video version of this tutorial.

item.title: JavaFX Fonts
item.description: The JavaFX Font class enables you to load and use different fonts in your JavaFX applications.

item.title: Java Exception Handling
item.description: I have updated my introduction to Java Exception Handling - the way you handle errors and exceptions in Java.

item.title: Race Conditions in Java
item.description: I have updated the Race Conditions in Java tutorial, with a video version among other changes.

item.title: JavaFX Pane
item.description: The JavaFX Pane class is a simple layout container which lays out its child components at exactly their layoutX and layoutY property values.

item.title: JavaFX Region
item.description: The JavaFX Region class is a base class for several JavaFX layout container classes like Pane, HBox and VBox. The JavaFX Region properties are thus shared by these subclasses.

item.title: JavaFX TableView
item.description: The JavaFX TableView is able to display tabular data in a grid-like table. I have updated this JavaFX TableView tutorial to also explain how to use Java Map instances as data items in a TableView.

item.title: Veteran, Senior and Junior Developers
item.description: In this new video I explain why I believe there is a developer experience level beyond senior developer, which I refer to as veteran developer.

item.title: Maven Memory Limits
item.description: You can set the memory limits of Maven when building your Maven projects. This Maven memory limits tutorial explains how.

item.title: Maven Surefire
item.description: The Maven Surefire plugin executes your Maven project's unit tests. This Maven Surefire tutorial has been updated to explain how to execute unit tests with Java preview features enabled.

item.title: Java Path in Java NIO
item.description: The Java NIO Path class represents a file system path. The Path class can help you do various path related calculations, such as expressing one Path as a relative path from another Path.

item.title: Java Comparator
item.description: The Java Comparator interface represents a component that is able to compare two objects, so the objects can be sorted using Java's built-in sorting functionality.

item.title: Java Comparable
item.description: The Java Comparable interface is intended to make your classes comparable, so you can sort them using Java's built-in sorting functionality.

item.title: JavaFX Stage Events
item.description: The JavaFX Stage class represents a window in a JavaFX app. The JavaFX Stage tutorial has been updated with a section about which events you can listen for on a JavaFX Stage (close requested, showing, shown, hiding, hidden).

item.title: Java ThreadLocal
item.description: A Java ThreadLocal is a class that can store one value internally per thread accessing it. I have updated the JavaThreadLocal tutorial, with a video version among other things.

item.title: Java 15 Features
item.description: I have updated my Java tutorial intro to list the new features in Java 15.

item.title: JavaFX Node
item.description: The JavaFX Node class is the base class (superclass) of all nodes (objects / components) added to the JavaFX scene graph. In this JavaFX Node tutorial I explain the basic properties of the JavaFX Node class.

item.title: Java volatile tutorial
item.description: I have updated the Java volatile tutorial - with a video version among other things.

item.title: JavaFX Intro Update
item.description: JavaFX is an advanced, hardware accelerated GUI toolkit for Java with new releases every 6 months. I have updated my introduction to JavaFX to be up-to-date.

item.title: Java AtomicLong
item.description: The Java AtomicLong is a concurrency utility that provides atomic operations on an internal long variable. I have updated this Java AtomicLong tutorial with a few details.

item.title: Java Synchronized
item.description: The Java synchronized tutorial has been updated - with a video version of the tutorial among other things.

item.title: Java Happens Before Guarantee
item.description: The Java happens before guarantee provides some restrictions on allowed instruction reordering for the Java VM and CPU. This Java happens before guarantee tutorial explains what these restrictions are. The Java happens before guarantee is actually part of the Java memory model, by the way.

item.title: Java Memory Model
item.description: The Java Memory Model specifies how the Java VM and threads access memory, and where local variables and objects are stored. I have updated this tutorial - among other things with a video version.

item.title: Java Modules
item.description: I have updated my Java Modules tutorial about the Java Platform Module System.

item.title: Build Java modules with Ant
item.description: This tutorial shows how to build Java modules (Java Platform Module System modules) with Ant.

item.title: Java Threads - Create, start and stop threads in Java
item.description: The tutorial about creating and starting threads in Java has just been updated to be a bit more precise, including a new recording of the video for the topic.

item.title: Java Concurrency and Multithreading
item.description: The Java Concurrency and multithreading introduction has been updated, among other things with links to video versions of some of the concurrency tutorials - one of which is an introduction to concurrency and multithreading.

item.title: Jackson JsonNode
item.description: The Jackson JsonNode represents a node in a JSON object graph. I have updated the Jackson JsonNode tutorial to explain how to iterate all fields or field names of a JsonNode.

item.title: Java Iterable
item.description: I have updated my Java Iterable tutorial - among other things with an explanation of the forEach() method which is an alternative way to iterate the elements of a Java Iterable.

item.title: RION Ops RionObjectWriter
item.description: The RionObjectWriter can serialize Java objects into RION - our fast, compact, versatile binary data format. The RionObjectWriter tutorial now explains how to write both acyclic and cyclic object graphs into RION.

item.title: Java Iterator
item.description: The Java Iterator tutorial has been updated to cover everything you need to know about the Iterator interface. Did you know about the Iterator forEachRemaining() method?

item.title: Java Stack Tutorial
item.description: The Java Stack class is a class stack implementation like you know them from an algorithms and data structures class. The Java Stack tutorial has received another round of updates, so it is now more precise.

item.title: RION Ops RionObjectReader
item.description: The RionObjectReader can read RION Object fields representing single objects or acyclic and/or cyclic object graphs.

item.title: Java Record
item.description: A Java Record is a class representing an immutable data record using a concise syntax. Java Records is a new feature in Java 14. This Java Record tutorial explains how records work.

item.title: Java Collections
item.description: I have updated my Java Collections introduction, with among other things a link to my Java Collections tutorial video playlist.

item.title: RION Ops - RionReader
item.description: The RION Ops for Java RionReader component enables you to read RION data fields reasonably easily, and with high performance. The RionReader tutorial now explains how to traverse a RION Table field.

item.title: RION Ops - RionWriter
item.description: The RION Ops for Java RionWriter component enables you to write RION data fields reasonably easily, and with high performance. Now this tutorial also explains how to write RION Table fields.

item.title: RION Ops - Introduction
item.description: Following release 0.11.0 of RION Ops, the Java toolkit for working with our compact, fast, versatile data format RION, I have updated the introduction to the RION Ops toolkit. More updates coming!

item.title: Java Deque
item.description: The Java Deque interface represents a double ended queue. The Java Deque tutorial has received a major update, including a video version of the tutorial.

item.title: Java 13 String Methods
item.description: The Java String tutorial has been updated to explain the new preview methods added in Java 13.

item.title: JavaFX GridPane
item.description: The JavaFX GridPane is a layout container that lays out its children in a grid. The JavaFX GridPane tutorial has been updated.

item.title: JavaFX Effects
item.description: JavaFX effects are graphical effects you can apply to a JavaFX Node - for instance blur, drop shadow, reflection etc. This JavaFX Effects introduction guides you through the basics of how to apply effects in your JavaFX applications.

item.title: Java Queue Tutorial Updated
item.description: The Java Queue tutorial has received a major update, including a video version of the tutorial too.

item.title: HTML5 File API
item.description: The HTML5 File API tutorial has been updated with information about how to upload a file from the browser via the new fetch() AJAX function in JavaScript.

item.title: JavaFX Text
item.description: The JavaFX Text control is used to display text inside your JavaFX application. This JavaFX tutorial explains the basics of the JavaFX Text control.

item.title: JavaFX Color
item.description: The JavaFX Color class and related classes are used to set e.g. the background color, border color etc of JavaFX controls. This JavaFX Color tutorial explains how to create JavaFX Color instances using various different color codes (web, RGB, HSB).

item.title: Maven Commands
item.description: Maven Commands tutorial updated - to include an example of how to skip unit tests during build (sometimes useful).

item.title: Java Map Video Tutorial
item.description: I have published a new video tutorial about the Java Map interface.

item.title: SVG Tutorial
item.description: I have updated the SVG tutorial introduction, and among other things made it more mobile friendly.

item.title: JavaFX Concurrency
item.description: This JavaFX Concurrency tutorial introduces how threads and concurrency works in JavaFX.

item.title: Java Concurrency
item.description: Java Concurrency introduction has been updated.

item.title: Java Concurrency Utils
item.description: Java Concurrency Utils introduction has been updated.

item.title: Java Set Tutorial Video
item.description: I have created a video version of my Java Set tutorial - for anyone who prefers video over text.

item.title: Java instanceof
item.description: The Java instanceof operator is used to determine if a given Java object is an instance of a specific class or interface. In Java 14 the Java instanceof operator has been extended with new, pattern matching functionality that makes it more concise to use when downcasting after an instanceof check.

item.title: Java Synchronized
item.description: The Java synchronized tutorial has been updated to include a lot more details about how synchronized blocks in Java works.

item.title: Java Map
item.description: The Java Map tutorial has been updated to promote the use of generic types (Java Generics) with maps.

item.title: Java Set
item.description: The Java Set tutorial has been updated to promote the use of generic types (Java Generics) with sets.

item.title: Java List
item.description: The Java List tutorial has been updated to promote the use of generic types (Java Generics) with lists.

item.title: Java Text Blocks
item.description: Java Text Blocks is a new feature in Java 13. I have updated the Java String tutorial to explain how the new Java text block feature works. A Java text block is also called a multi line String.

item.title: Your First Java App
item.description: I have updated the tutorial about how to create your first Java app to use IntelliJ IDEA Community Edition 2019.3.3 and Java 13.

item.title: Installing the Java SDK
item.description: I have updated the tutorial about how to install the Java SDK to match the reality from Java 13 and forward.

item.title: What is Java?
item.description: I have updated the What is Java tutorial to reflect what Java is as of Java 13 and forward.

item.title: Java AtomicStampedReference
item.description: I have updated the Java AtomicStampedRefence tutorial somewhat. The AtomicStampedReference can be used to avoid the classic A-B-A concurrency problem.

item.title: Java switch Expression Improvements in Java 13
item.description: I have updated the Java switch tutorial to explain the Java switch expression improvements in Java 13.

item.title: Maven Java Compiler Version
item.description: This tutorial explains how to set the Java compiler version to compile your project with, inside the POM file for the project. The tutorial now explains how to enable the preview features in Java 12 and 13.

item.title: CSS display
item.description: The CSS display property specifies how an HTML element is to be rendered (displayed) in the HTML element flow of an HTML page. The CSS display tutorial has been updated to cover more valid property values.

item.title: Java Threads
item.description: Java Threads are like virtual CPUs that can execute parts of your application in parallel. I have updated the tutorial about how to create and start threads in Java.

item.title: JavaFX HBox
item.description: The JavaFX HBox control is a layout control which lays out its child nodes next to each other on a horizontal row. The JavaFX HBox tutorial has been updated with information about child node margins plus the hgrow and fillHeight properties.

item.title: Java Map
item.description: The Java Map tutorial has been corrected to say that null keys are allowed in a Java Map. Counter-intuitive - but true.

item.title: JavaFX VBox
item.description: The JavaFX VBox can layout its child components in a vertical column - on top of each other. The JavaFX VBox tutorial has been updated to explain concepts like fillWidth, CSS styling, margins around child nodes etc.

item.title: Java Resizable Array
item.description: I have updated the Java Resizable Array tutorial to explain why a Java ArrayList cannot always do the job (performance reasons).

item.title: RION Ops RionObjectWriter
item.description: The RionObjectReader makes it easy to write Java objects out to RION. To serialize Java objects as RION, in other words.

item.title: RION Ops RionObjectReader
item.description: The RionObjectReader makes it easy to read RION data into Java objects. To deserialize Java objects from RION, in other words.

item.title: New in Java 14
item.description: I hve updated the Java introduction to list what is new in Java 14 which is due for release in March 2020.

item.title: Java Stack
item.description: The Java Stack tutorial has been updated to show examples with generic types, and mentioning the use of Deque as a more modern alternative to Stack.

item.title: Tutorials - New Layout
item.description: We are currently experimenting with the layout of tutorials.jenkov.com, so if the layout looks a bit weird - please be patient. We will fix it soon. If not, let us know, and we will fix it.

item.title: Java System.in, System.out and System.err
item.description: The Java System.in, System.out and System.err streams provide a simple way to send data into a Java application, and output data from a Java app too. This tutorial has been clarified.

item.title: RION Encoding
item.description: The RION Encoding tutorial has been updated to be more clear, and to also contain encoding examples in hexadecimal notation, so you can now see exactly what RION encoded data looks like.

item.title: RION Design Goals
item.description: RION was designed to be fast, compact, expressive, suitable as network protocol message format, as well as a few other goals. This text explains the design goals of RION.

item.title: RION Introduction
item.description: RION is a fast, compact, versatile, binary data format. RION was designed for data exchange, but works fine as a data storage format too. The RION introduction has been extended and improved.

item.title: Jackson JsonNode Traversal
item.description: The Jackson JsonNode tutorial has been extended to explain how to traverse a JsonNode graph representing either a JSON object or JSON Array.

item.title: Concurrency vs Parallelism
item.description: Concurrency and parallelism are two commonly used term when talking about multithreading - but what do they mean exactly? This tutorial explains the difference. The explanations have been updated to be more clear.

item.title: Java File
item.description: The Java File tutorial has been updated. The Java File class gives you access to file system operations like renaming or deleting files or directories, check if a file exists, list directory content etc.

item.title: Java DataInputStream
item.description: The Java DataInputStream enables you to read primitive data types (byte, short int, float etc) from an underlying InputStream. The Java DataInputStream tutorial has been updated to explain more of its available read methods.

item.title: Same-threading
item.description: Same-threading is single-threaded design scaled out to N threads. I have updated my same-threading tutorial to explain this more precisely.

item.title: Java RandomAccessFile
item.description: The Java RandomAccessFile class enables you to read from and write to a file at arbitrary locations. In other words, you can move freely around the file, both forth and back, and read from or write to the file at the desired location. I have updated the Java RandomAccessFile tutorial to explain better how to use it.

item.title: Stream Processing API Designs
item.description: Having studied various different stream processing APIs over the last 1,5 years, I have written up an analysis of what to look for when choosing a stream processing API.

item.title: Java ZipFile - Zip Slip Attack
item.description: I have updated the Java ZipFile tutorial to explain how to avoid the Zip Slip attack !

item.title: JavaFX TableView
item.description: The JavaFX TableView enables you to display data in a tabular control. The TableView tutorial has been updated to explain the TableView SelectionModel - how rows are selected in the TableView.

item.title: Concurrency Models
item.description: Over time the developer community has come up with several different concurrency models, each with their own pros and cons. This concurrency model tutorial has been updated to explain the shared state and separate state variations of a concurrency model.

item.title: JavaFX Button
item.description: The JavaFX Button represents a visual button the user can click to perform some action. The JavaFX Button tutorial has been updated with information about how to disable a button so it is visible but not clickable.

item.title: Java Concurrency Tutorial
item.description: I have updated the Java Concurrency tutorial introduction to be more up-to-date. In general I will be updating the tutorials in this trail over the coming months.

item.title: Java ByteArrayOutputStream
item.description: The Java ByteArrayOutputStream can collect the bytes written to and return them as a Java byte array. Thus, the Java ByteArrayOutputStream is handy when you have a component that can only write to an OutputStream, but you need the data as a byte array. This Java ByteArrayOutputStream tutorial has been updated with more descriptions and examples.

item.title: Java ByteArrayInputStream
item.description: The Java ByteArrayInputStream can turn a Java byte array into an InputStream. Thus, the Java ByteArrayInputStream is very useful when you have a byte array, but need an InputStream for some purpose. This Java ByteArrayInputStream tutorial has been updated with more descriptions and examples.

item.title: Java ThreadLocal
item.description: The Java ThreadLocal provides a variable where values set can only be read by the same thread that set them. The Java ThreadLocal tutorial has now been updated with more information about setting initial value, removing the value etc.

item.title: Java Collections Tutorial
item.description: Over the course of the last year, most of the tutorials in the Java Collections tutorial series have been updated, and 3 new tutorials have been added.

item.title: Java NavigableMap
item.description: The Java NavigableMap tutorial has been cleaned up with more descriptions and more examples.

item.title: Java BufferedReader
item.description: The Java BufferedReader tutorial has been updated with information about how to implement your own reusable BufferedReader.

item.title: Java 13 Features
item.description: The Java tutorial introduction has been updated with a list of the new features in Java 13.

item.title: Java BufferedInputStream
item.description: The Java BufferedInputStream tutorial has been updated with an example of how to create a reusable BufferedInputStream.

item.title: Mem Ops Bytes
item.description: The Mem Ops Bytes class represents a byte sequence allocated from a shared byte array. Bytes is designed for pooling and freeing of both itself and the allocated bytes.

item.title: Mem Ops 0.6.2
item.description: Mem Ops 0.6.2 is out, which has more advanced pooling of Bytes objects which represents byte sequences allocated in a shared byte array.

item.title: RION Ops in Central Maven Repo
item.description: RION Ops for Java- an open source toolkit for reading and writing the compact, fast binary RION data format, is now available in the Maven central repo.

item.title: Maven Java Compiler - Java 9 and Later
item.description: The Maven Java Compiler tutorial has been updated to explain how to set the Java compiler for Java 9 and later.

item.title: Java SortedMap
item.description: The Java SortedMap is like a Map where the keys are kept sorted internally, so the keys can be iterated later in sort order, or reverse sort order.

item.title: Mem Ops for Java Available in Maven Central Repo
item.description: Mem Ops for Java, the open source toolkit for memory allocation in apps that require stable memory consumption under high load, is now available in the central Maven repository. The Mem Ops for Java tutorial now describes the Maven coordinates required to use Mem Ops with Maven.

item.title: Dockerfile Tutorial
item.description: The Docker Dockerfile contains the recipe for how to build a Docker image. You can ask Docker to execute a Dockerfile and build a Docker image as a result.

item.title: Java NavigableSet
item.description: The Java NavigableSet is a sub-interface of Java SortedSet, with a few extra navigation methods. The Java NavigableSet tutorial has been updated.

item.title: Docker Tutorial
item.description: Docker containers are an alternative to virtual machines which have less overhead, and which can be created from a Dockerfile (configuration file), so you can recreate the container again and again.

item.title: Java PushbackInputStream
item.description: The Java PushbackInputStream enables you to push back bytes into the PushbackInputStream which has been read from it - for later re-reading. This is useful when parsing binary data formats.

item.title: Java PushbackReader
item.description: The Java PushbackReader enables you to push back characters into the PushbackReader which has been read from it - for later re-reading. This is useful when parsing text or textual data formats.

item.title: RION Ops RionReader
item.description: The RION Ops RionReader is able to read RION fields, e.g. written with the RionWriter, into various Java data types.

item.title: RION Ops RionWriter
item.description: The RION Ops RionWriter is able to write various types of data as RION fields into a destination byte array. These RION Fields can later be read using the RION Ops RionReader.

item.title: RION Ops
item.description: RION Ops is an open source toolkit to work with the compact, fast, binary data format RION.

item.title: RION Data Format
item.description: The RION data format is a binary, compact, fast general purpose data format. RION was formerly known as ION, but has been renamed to distinguish it from Amazon's ION data format.

item.title: Java SortedSet
item.description: The Java SortedSet is a Set which keeps its element sorted internally, so they can be iterated in the sorted order.

item.title: Jackson ObjectMapper
item.description: The Jackson ObjectMapper is one of the most central classes of the Jackson JSON parser / generator API. The Jackson ObjectMapper tutorial has been updated with information about how to convert from JsonNode to objects, and from objects to JsonNode instances.

item.title: Jackson JsonNode
item.description: The Jackson JsonNode class is a dynamic representation of a JSON object. The JsonNode tutorial has received a major round of updates.

item.title: Build a Fat JAR With Maven
item.description: You can configure Maven to build a Fat JAR from your project containing all your project's compiled classes, and all classes of all dependencies your project uses, in single JAR file called a Fat JAR. Very useful for packaging microservices, executable JARs etc. - also inside a Docker container.

item.title: Java InputStreamReader
item.description: The Java InputStreamReader tutorial has received a major round of updates. The InputStreamReader turns a byte based InputStream into a character based Reader.

item.title: Java InputStream
item.description: The Java InputStream tutorial has received a major round of updates. InputStream represents an inbound stream of bytes.

item.title: Java IO Tutorial
item.description: The Java IO Tutorial has been updated in several places. Many of the core texts have been updated and some almost completely rewritten.

item.title: Jackson JsonNode
item.description: The Jackson JsonNode tutorial has been updated with a description about how to access fields using a JSON path expression (like XPath) via the at() method.

item.title: Java Properties Tutorial
item.description: The Java Properties tutorial has received a major round of updates, covering more advanced topics of the Java Properties class, like default properties, loading from classpath etc.

item.title: The Store and Process Stream Processing Design Pattern
item.description: The store and process stream processing design pattern is a simple design which is easy to implement and which can scale nicely, both in terms of complexity of stream processing logic and performance.

item.title: JavaFX HTMLEditor
item.description: The JavaFX HTMLEditor enables the user to edit HTML in a wysiwyg editor.

item.title: JavaFX Pagination
item.description: The JavaFX Pagination control enables the user to navigate page by page through content, e.g. search results, articles, images etc.

item.title: JavaFX ColorPicker
item.description: A JavaFX ColorPicker enables the user to choose a color from a small wizard-like popup dialog.

item.title: JavaFX Separator
item.description: The JavaFX Separator shows a visual divider between JavaFX controls inside a container, e.g. inside a VBox or HBox.

item.title: JavaFX ContextMenu
item.description: A JavaFX ContextMenu provides a right click menu which can be attached to a JavaFX control. This JavaFX ContextMenu tutorial explains how to use it.

item.title: Stream Ops StreamOps Facade Class
item.description: The StreamOps class in the Stream Ops data streaming API is a convenience facade class that can act as a starting point when using Stream Ops.

item.title: JavaFX SplitMenuButton
item.description: The JavaFX SplitMenuButton is an advanced MenuButton which can have an extra, often used menu choice available to click on directly, as a side-mounted button.

item.title: JavaFX ScrollPane
item.description: A JavaFX ScrollPane is a container that has two scrollbars around the component it contains if the component is larger than the visible area of the ScrollPane. The scrollbars enable the user to scroll around the component shown inside the ScrollPane, so different parts of the component can be seen.

item.title: Java Properties
item.description: The Java Properties class is a simple key,value store, like a Hashtable. The Java Properties class can write properties to disk and read them again, in both property file format and XML format.

item.title: Stream Ops Introduction
item.description: We have begun the documentation of Stream Ops - the embeddable data streaming engine for Java. Stream Ops enable you to write data records into a stream which is stored on disk, and read the records in the same order again later.

item.title: Compare and Swap in Java
item.description: Compare and swap is an essential operation in concurrency libraries. This tutorial explains the idea behind compare and swap, and how Java provides compare and swap functionality.

item.title: JDBC Batch Update
item.description: JDBC batch updates can batch multiple SQL statements into a batch which is sent to the database in a single batch, speeding up the update execution. The JDBC batch update tutorial has just been updated.

item.title: JavaFX SplitPane
item.description: JavaFX SplitPane is a container component which splits its screen space between two or more controls. Between each two controls is a divider which can be moved by the user to specify how much space each control should take up within the SplitPane.

item.title: JavaFX TabPane
item.description: JavaFX TabPane is a container component which can contain multiple sections (tabs) which can be displayed by clicking on the tab above the section area.

item.title: Parsing and Formatting Dates in Java
item.description: Parsing and formatting dates in Java can be done using different classes. This updated tutorial gives a short overview of the options Java provides for parsing and formatting dates.

item.title: Java switch Expression
item.description: Java 12 added the experimental Java switch expression which enables switch statements to return a value. I have expanded the Java switch tutorial to explain the Java switch expression.

item.title: Mem Ops ObjectPool
item.description: The Mem Ops ObjectPool component is an object pool which allows you to obtain objects which can be freed back to the object pool for later reuse.

item.title: JavaFX Slider
item.description: The JavaFX Slider control is enables the user to select a value within a preset interval by sliding a handle from left to right.

item.title: Java Ternary Operator
item.description: The Java Ternary Operator provides a simplified if-statement construct. The Java Ternary Operator tutorial has been updated to show how to chain more than one ternary operator into a single expression.

item.title: JavaFX ProgressBar
item.description: The JavaFX ProgressBar control is capable of displaying the progress of some task inside a bar.

item.title: Mem Ops BytesAllocatorManualDefrag
item.description: The Mem Ops BytesAllocatorManualDefrag is a byte block allocator which can allocate smaller blocks of bytes from a bigger byte array. The allocated blocks need to be explicitly freed again to be reallocated. You will need to manually trigger defragmentation of the free blocks when you find it suitable.

item.title: Java Collections Class
item.description: The Java Collections class contains several useful utility methods for working with Java Collections, like Lists etc.

item.title: JavaFX Hyperlink
item.description: The JavaFX Hyperlink is a control that displays a text which can be clicked like a button, just like a link in a web page. Your JavaFX application can respond to clicks on a JavaFX Hyperlink, just like on a Button.

item.title: Mem Ops BytesAllocatorAutoDefrag
item.description: The Mem Ops BytesAllocatorAutoDefrag enables you to allocate smaller sections of a bigger byte array, and free them again. When freed, the BytesAllocatorAutoDefrag automatically defragments the free blocks of the bigger byte array.

item.title: Mem Ops
item.description: Mem Ops is a small open source toolkit which provides tools for memory management for Java applications that need to allocate and deallocate byte arrays or objects at a rapid pace.

item.title: JavaFX Accordion
item.description: The JavaFX Accordion is a control which can contain several sections of content, each of which can be expanded and collapsed.

item.title: Java Streams API
item.description: The Java Streams API tutorial has been updated a bit, including a video version of part of the material now.

item.title: Java Streams API - Video Tutorial
item.description: I have published a video about basics of the Java Streams API - for those who prefer a screencast over reading text.

item.title: Java SimpleDateFormat
item.description: Java SimpleDateFormat tutorial updated. The SimpleDateFormat class is used to parse date strings into Date objects, and format Date objects into strings.

item.title: JavaFX TitledPane
item.description: The JavaFX TitledPane is a container control which displays a title above its content. The TitledPane can be collapsed and expanded to hide and show its content.

item.title: UTF-8
item.description: UTF-8 is one of the most popular encodings used for Unicode characters. This UTF-8 tutorial explains how to encode and decode Unicode characters (code points) to and from UTF-8.

item.title: Unicode
item.description: Unicode is an international standard for how to represent characters of many different alphabets as bytes in a computer. This new unicode tutorial explains the basics of unicode.

item.title: Data Streaming Storage Calculations
item.description: When implementing data streaming it is useful to know how much storage the solution requires. This tutorial explains how to calculate (approximately) how much storage your data streaming solution will use.

item.title: Java Stack
item.description: I have updated my Java Stack tutorial. A Java Stack is a data structure where you push element on, and pop element off the top of the stack, like a stack of plates.

item.title: Software Elegance
item.description: Following some feedback from readers I have added a few more sections and an example to my beginning trail on functional software elegance.

item.title: JavaFX TreeTableView
item.description: The JavaFX TreeTableView enables you to display trees in JavaFX, and for each node in the tree you can have a row of column values displayed next to it.

item.title: JavaFX TreeView
item.description: The JavaFX TreeView enables you to display trees which can have the nodes expanded and collapsed inside your JavaFX applications.

item.title: Java Collection Interface
item.description: I have updated my Java Collection interface tutorial. The Java Collection interface is one of the core interfaces in the Java Collections API.

item.title: Software Elegance
item.description: I have formalized my guiding sentence containing my three rules for achieving software elegance - functionally elegant software - in this latest tutorial touching the subject of usability.

item.title: CSS Tutorial
item.description: I have updated my introduction to CSS as a starting point for updating several of the topics in the CSS tutorial.

item.title: Java Arrays
item.description: A Java array is a collection of variables of the same data type which can be access individually, or traversed one by one. As part of my Java tutorial overhaul, I have given the Java Array tutorial an update too.

item.title: New in Java 12
item.description: Java 12 is out soon, so I have updated the Java tutorial to list the new features in Java 12.

item.title: Java JDBC Connection
item.description: A JDBC Connection is used to connect to a relational database from a Java application. I have updated the JDBC Connection tutorial to cover more of its core methods.

item.title: Jackson JsonNode
item.description: The Jackson JsonNode is Jackson's tree model for representing dynamic JSON object graphs in a Java application. This Jackson JsonNode tutorial explains how to get and set fields etc. on a JsonNode.

item.title: JavaFX TableView
item.description: The JavaFX TableView class is used to display tabular data inside a JavaFX application. In this JavaFX TableView tutorial I explain how to use the TableView.

item.title: JDBC Tutorial
item.description: I have updated the JDBC tutorial introduction quite a bit. Now it contains a list of popular relational databases, a full JDBC example, and a better explanation of what JDBC is intended for, among other things.

item.title: Joining Data Streams
item.description: Joining records from different data streams can be very useful, but also complicates your data streaming pipelines. This data stream joining tutorial explores what to look out for when joining data streams.

item.title: Java Annotations
item.description: The Java Annotations tutorial has been updated, and a video version of the tutorial embedded.

item.title: Jackson ObjectMapper - CBOR and MessagePack
item.description: The Jackson ObjectMapper can be used to read and write CBOR and MessagePack encoded data too - two compact binary data formats.

item.title: JavaFX DirectoryChooser
item.description: The JavaFX DirectoryChooser is a dialog which enables the user to choose a directory. Your JavaFX application can then read what directory the user chose.

item.title: Data Streaming Scalability
item.description: Scaling a data stream up is easy, but once you scale it out, certain distribution effects need to be taken into consideration.

item.title: Java 11 Features
item.description: I have added a list of the most important Java 11 features to my Java tutorial introduction.

item.title: Java Iterator
item.description: The Java Iterator tutorial has been updated with information about the ConcurrentModificationException and remove() method.

item.title: Java Try With Resources
item.description: The Java Try With Resources tutorial has been improved a lot. It explains how to have resources automatically closed after use, when used inside a Java try-with-resources construct.

item.title: Data Streaming Products
item.description: I have compiled a list of data streaming products, and shortly explained what categories of products they belong to.

item.title: JavaFX Scene
item.description: The JavaFX Scene object represents the root of the content area of a JavaFX Stage (application window).

item.title: JavaFX Stage
item.description: The JavaFX Stage object represents an application window in a JavaFX application.

item.title: Java Functional Composition
item.description: Functional composition in Java enables you to compose new functions by combining existing functions into a single, new function.

item.title: Data Streaming
item.description: Data Streaming is a data distribution technique where data producers write data records into an ordered data stream from which data consumers can read the records in the same order.

item.title: Java Stream API
item.description: The Java Stream API provides a functional approach to processing streams of objects - also known as functional stream processing.

item.title: Java ExecutorService
item.description: The Java ExecutorService can execute tasks concurrently in separate threads. I have added information about how to cancel tasks, plus a few other changes.

item.title: Java Future
item.description: The Java Future interface represents the result of a task that is executed concurrently, for instance by a Java ExecutorService.

item.title: Java Callable
item.description: The Java Callable interface represents a task that can be executed concurrently, for instance by a Java ExecutorService.

item.title: JavaFX WebView
item.description: The JavaFX WebView enables you to embed web pages or web applications inside your JavaFX applications. A handy way to combine web and desktop apps!

item.title: Java Ternary Operator
item.description: I had completely forgotten the Java ternary operator in my basic Java tutorial! Well - now it is included.

item.title: New in Java 10
item.description: Did you miss what was new in Java 10? The Java tutorial has been updated to list the most important changes.

item.title: Java List
item.description: The Java List tutorial has received a major round of improvements! More will come in a near future.

item.title: Java Set
item.description: The Java Set tutorial has been updated extensively! More to come soon!

item.title: Acme4J Tutorial
item.description: Acme4J is a Java toolkit for obtaining automatically issued free TLS / SSL certificates from the Let's Encrypt Certificate Authority.

item.title: Java Map
item.description: Java Map deep dive! Big round of updates. Notice especially the functional operations added in Java 8.

item.title: Java Functional Interfaces
item.description: Java 8 comes with a set of built-in functional interfaces which can be used with e.g. the Streams API. This tutorial explains some of the core functional interfaces in Java.

item.title: Maven Java Compiler Version
item.description: You pretty much always have to set the Java compiler in your Maven POM files. Now the Maven tutorial explains how.

item.title: Java Interfaces
item.description: Java interfaces can contain static methods. I have extended my Java interfaces tutorial to include that.

item.title: Higher Order Functions in Java
item.description: I have extended the Java functional programming tutorial with a text explaining higher order functions and giving examples in Java.

item.title: Java Functional Programming
item.description: I have started a new tutorial series on Java functional programming. Basic concepts explained. More will come.

item.title: Java Enum
item.description: The Java enum tutorial has been updated with several new sections, including a video version.

item.title: Java Lambda Expressions
item.description: The Java lambda expression tutorial has been updated quite a bit, and now includes a video version too.

item.title: JavaFX FileChooser
item.description: A JavaFX FileChooser is a dialog that enables the user to select one or more files via a file explorer from the user's local computer.

item.title: JavaFX ToolTip
item.description: JavaFX ToolTip is a small popup with explanatory text shown when a user hovers the mouse over a JavaFX control.

item.title: JavaFX ToolBar
item.description: JavaFX ToolBar is a horizontal or vertical bar of buttons and other JavaFX components typically used to select or interact with different tools.

item.title: JavaFX FXML
item.description: JavaFX FXML is an XML format used to compose JavaFX GUIs in a way similar to how you compose web GUIs with HTML.

item.title: Java volatile
item.description: The Java volatile tutorial has been updated, so the visibility and happens-before guarantee is more properly explained.

item.title: Java Modules
item.description: The biggest addition to Java 9 is the Java Platform Module System which brings better modularity to Java. This Java modules tutorial explains how modules work.

item.title: JavaFX MenuBar
item.description: The JavaFX MenuBar adds a top menu to a JavaFX application, just like you are used to from desktop applications.

item.title: Java Certificate
item.description: The Java Certificate class represents a cryptographic identity certificate, possibly signed by a Certificate Authority (CA).

item.title: Java KeyPair
item.description: The Java KeyPair class represents a public key, private key pair used in asymmetric encryption (e.g. RSA).

item.title: Java KeyPairGenerator
item.description: The Java KeyPairGenerator can generate asymmetric encryption key pairs (public key, private key). This Java KeyPairGenerator tutorial explains how to use the KeyPairGenerator class.

item.title: Java KeyGenerator
item.description: The Java KeyGenerator can generate symmetric encryption keys. This Java KeyGenerator tutorial explains how to use the KeyGenerator class.

item.title: Java Signature
item.description: The Java Signature class can create digital signatures from binary data. This Java Signature tutorial explains how to create and verify digital signatures.

item.title: Java Keytool
item.description: The Java Keytool is a command line tool that can work with Java KeyStore files - e.g. generate key pairs into a KeyStore, import / export certificates etc.

item.title: Java MessageDigest
item.description: The Java MessageDigest class can calculate a message digest (cryptographic hash value) from a block of data.

item.title: Java Cipher
item.description: The Java Cipher class represents an encryption algorithm and can encrypt and decrypt data.

item.title: Java KeyStore
item.description: The Java KeyStore is an encrypted, password protected store for encryption keys.

item.title: Java Cryptography
item.description: The first part of a new Java Cryptography tutorial has been released!

item.title: Java Regex - Updated
item.description: The Java Regex (regular expressions) tutorial has been updated to better introduce how regular expressions work.

item.title: Java String - Updated
item.description: The Java String tutorial has been updated with explanations of its regular expression methods.

item.title: Jackson ObjectMapper - Updated
item.description: The Jackson ObjectMapper tutorial (Java JSON toolkit) has received a major update!

item.title: Netty ChannelPipeline
item.description: The Netty ChannelPipeline contains ChannelHandlers that process incoming and outgoing data from sockets.

item.title: Netty TCP client
item.description: This tutorial explains how to create a TCP client with Netty.

item.title: Netty TCP Server
item.description: This tutorial explains how to create a TCP server with Netty.

item.title: Netty Overview
item.description: An overview of Netty for developers getting started with Netty.

item.title: Riot.js Conditionals
item.description: Riot.js conditionals can show, hide or remove HTML elements depending on the condition specified.

item.title: Riot.js Expressions
item.description: Riot.js expressions enable you to generate HTML based on JavaScript variables. This tutorial was just updated.

item.title: Riot.js
item.description: Riot.js is a small JavaScript web framework centered around the ideas of React.js and web components.

item.title: ION Specification
item.description: ION is a fast, compact, versatile binary data format similar to binary JSON.

item.title: Java GZIPOutputStream Tutorial
item.description: The Java GZIPOutputStream class can GZIP compress data and write it to an OutputStream.

item.title: Java GZIPInputStream Tutorial
item.description: The Java GZIPInputStream class can read a GZIP compressed file as a normal InputStream - decompressed.

item.title: Java ZipFile Tutorial
item.description: The Java ZipFile class can read files contained in a ZIP file. This tutorial explains how to use the ZipFile class.

item.title: ModRun Tutorial
item.description: ModRun brings modularity to Java without project Jigsaw. Load classes directly from Maven repositories!

item.title: Race Conditions - Fixed!
item.description: A significant error at the end of this articles was fixed.

item.title: JavaFX StackedAreaChart
item.description: The JavaFX StackedAreaChart component can stacked draw area charts inside your JavaFX applications.

item.title: JavaFX AreaChart
item.description: The JavaFX AreaChart component can draw area charts inside your JavaFX applications.

item.title: JavaFX LineChart
item.description: The JavaFX LineChart component can draw line charts inside your JavaFX applications.

item.title: JavaFX ScatterChart
item.description: The JavaFX ScatterChart component can draw scatter charts inside your JavaFX applications.

item.title: JavaFX StackedBarChart
item.description: The JavaFX StackedBarChart component can draw stacked bar charts inside your JavaFX applications.

item.title: JavaFX BarChart
item.description: The JavaFX BarChart component can draw bar charts inside your JavaFX applications.

item.title: JavaFX PieChart
item.description: The JavaFX PieChart component can draw pie charts inside your JavaFX applications.

item.title: Maven Unit Test Report
item.description: This tutorial explains how to generate unit test reports with Maven and the Surefire plugin.

item.title: JavaFX GridPane
item.description: The JavaFX GridPane can layout components in a grid. Each component can span multiple rows and columns.

item.title: JavaFX TilePane
item.description: The JavaFX TilePane can layout components in a horizontal or vertical grid of equally sized tiles.

item.title: JavaFX FlowPane
item.description: The JavaFX FlowPane can layout components in a horizontal or vertical flow, breaking onto next line when there is no more space in the width or height.

item.title: JavaFX Group
item.description: The JavaFX Group component groups a set of JavaFX components together so effects and transformations can be applied to the components as a group.

item.title: Butterfly DI Container
item.description: The documentation for Butterfly DI Container has been published on this tutorial website.

item.title: JavaFX TextArea
item.description: JavaFX TextArea enables the user to enter a text that spans multiple lines.

item.title: JavaFX PasswordField
item.description: JavaFX PasswordField enables the user to enter a password. The PasswordField hides what is entered - showing circles instead of characters.

item.title: JavaFX DatePicker
item.description: JavaFX DatePicker enables the user to enter a date or choose a date from a wizard-like popup dialog.

item.title: JavaFX ListView
item.description: JavaFX ListView enables the user to choose a value from a predefined list of choices.

item.title: JavaFX ComboBox
item.description: JavaFX ComboBox enables the user to choose a value from a predefined list of choices or type in a value of the user's own choice.

item.title: JavaFX ChoiceBox
item.description: JavaFX ChoiceBox enables the user to choose a value from a predefined list of choices.

item.title: JavaFX CheckBox
item.description: JavaFX CheckBox is a button that can be either selected, not selected or in an indeterminate state.

item.title: JavaFX RadioButton
item.description: JavaFX RadioButton is a button that can be either selected or not selected. As part of a toggle group only one RadioButton in the group can be selected.

item.title: JavaFX ToggleButton
item.description: JavaFX ToggleButton is a button that can be either pressed or not pressed, and can be part of a toggle group (group of toggle buttons).

item.title: JavaFX MenuButton
item.description: JavaFX MenuButton can display a list of menu items (options) the user can choose from, like a drop down menu.

item.title: JavaFX VBox
item.description: JavaFX VBox is a layout component which positions all its nested nodes (controls) in a vertical row.

item.title: JavaFX HBox
item.description: JavaFX HBox is a layout component which positions all its nested nodes (controls) in a horizontal row.

item.title: JavaFX TextField
item.description: JavaFX ImageField enables a user to enter text which can then be read by the JavaFX application.

item.title: JavaFX ImageView
item.description: JavaFX ImageView can display images inside a JavaFX application. They can also be used inside labels and buttons.

item.title: JavaFX Button
item.description: JavaFX buttons enables an application to react when the user clicks the button.

item.title: JavaFX Label
item.description: JavaFX labels can display text and images inside a JavaFX application.

item.title: Your First JavaFX Application
item.description: This text how to create a basic JavaFX application, so you can start playing with it.

item.title: JavaFX Overview
item.description: This text gives an overview of the JavaFX platform and how JavaFX applications are structured.

item.title: JavaFX Tutorial Started
item.description: JavaFX is an impressive GUI toolkit for Java which replaces Java Swing as recommended GUI platform.

item.title: Same-threading
item.description: A same-threaded system that scales a single-threaded design out on multiple CPUs.

item.title: Micro Batching
item.description: Micro batching is a technique that groups tasks into small batches to gain some of the throughput advantages of batching without sacrificing latency too much.

item.title: Java High Performance Read Patterns
item.description: This tutorial covers 4 common data read patterns in Java and discusses their performance.

item.title: Learning is Hard, But Don't Give Up
item.description: Learning new topics can often be hard and frustrating. In this article I explain how I face the challenge of learning.

item.title: Standard Deviation
item.description: The standard deviation of a data set is calculated as the square root of the variance. This standard deviation tutorial explains how to calculate standard deviation of a data set.

item.title: Variance
item.description: The variance of a data set is a measure of how much the observations in the data set deviate from the mean value of the data set. Variance is also used as base for calculating standard deviation.

item.title: Mean Deviation
item.description: The mean deviation tells how the observations of a data set are distributed around the mean value of the data set.

item.title: Frequency Distribution
item.description: The frequency distribution of a data set shows the number of occurrences of each value.

item.title: Range of a Data Set
item.description: The range of a data set is the difference between the highest and the lowest value. This tutorial explains range with an example.

item.title: Mode of a Data Set
item.description: The mode of a data set is the most frequently occurring value. This tutorial explains mode with an example.

item.title: Mathematical Analysis
item.description: We have started a tutorial about mathematical analysis which is a core part of data science.

item.title: Java JSON Tutorial - Part 2
item.description: We have now completed another big round of updates to our Java JSON tutorial.

item.title: GSON JsonParser
item.description: The GSON JsonParser can parse a JSON string or stream into a Java object tree structure.

item.title: GSON Tutorial Updated
item.description: The GSON tutorial has been updated with sections about custom serializers, deserializers and how to include null fields in the generated JSON.

item.title: GSON JsonReader
item.description: A short introduction to GSON's pull parser for parsing JSON. GSON is Google's Java JSON API.

item.title: IapObjectWriter Tutorial Updated
item.description: Information added about how to use different field names in, or exclude fields from ION serialization.

item.title: IapObjectWriter Tutorial Updated
item.description: Information added about how to use different field names in, or exclude fields from ION serialization.

item.title: GSON Tutorial Updated
item.description: The GSON tutorial has been updated with information about how to exclude fields from serialization / deserialization, pretty printing JSON etc.

item.title: HTML5 Local Storage Updated
item.description: The HTML5 local storage tutorial has been extended and polished.

item.title: JCTools Introduction
item.description: JCTools contains a set of high performance, non-blocking concurrent data structures missing in core Java.

item.title: Chronicle Introduction
item.description: Chronicle is a suite of high performance libraries to make it easier to process data in low latency, high throughput environments.

item.title: ION - Fast Binary Data Format
item.description: ION is a new, binary data format. ION is the default data format used in IAP - our new network protocol.

item.title: IAP - New Network Protocol
item.description: IAP is a new general purpose network protocol intended to replace HTTP which we are developing at http://vstack.co .

item.title: New Design!
item.description: This tutorial website has a new, faster, more mobile friendly design, which we believe to be more user friendly too!

item.title: Java ArrayList vs. OpenArrayList Performance
item.description: Did you know there is up to 12% difference in performance depending on how you iterate an ArrayList?
